name: JetBrains Air
description: An agentic development environment launched by JetBrains on March 9, 2026 as a public preview. Built on JetBrains' 26 years of experience, Air enables developers to delegate coding tasks to multiple AI agents that can run concurrently. It represents a new wave of dev tooling that orchestrates multiple AI coding agents, replacing JetBrains' abandoned Fleet IDE project.
community_quotes: Discussion on Hacker News about 'a new wave of dev tooling built on 26 years of experience'
26 points on Hacker News discussion thread
Built on the corpse of abandoned Fleet IDE

name: JetBrains Junie CLI
description: An LLM-agnostic AI coding agent by JetBrains that entered Beta in March 2026. Junie combines LLM capabilities with deep project context, structured understanding, and workflow integration. Available as a CLI tool and JetBrains IDE plugin, supporting models like Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3 Flash, and Codex 5.3. Includes up to $50 usage per user during beta.
community_quotes: Junie is disappointing and I'm glad I tried it before buying a blindly subscribing
Positive mentions of model-agnosticism and strong benchmark performance
Reviewers noting it works with fast, low-cost models
Grounded in your codebase with deep project context

name: ReSharper for Visual Studio Code
description: JetBrains launched the full version of ReSharper for Visual Studio Code on March 5, 2026. The extension brings gold-standard C# code analysis, refactorings, and navigation to VS Code without requiring the C# Dev Kit. Includes AI-assisted code review capabilities and comprehensive inspections for member renaming, signature modifications, and method extraction.
community_quotes: Over 2.5M+ downloads on Visual Studio Marketplace
Bringing trusted ReSharper refactorings to VS Code

name: Xcode 26.3
description: Apple's release of Xcode 26.3 on February 26, 2026, introducing native 'agentic coding' capabilities. The update allows developers to harness AI agents like Anthropic's Claude Agent and OpenAI's Codex directly within Xcode for autonomous code analysis, building, testing, and iteration. Features 20 built-in tools exposed via Model Context Protocol (MCP).
community_quotes: I am able to produce working non-trivial apps, which would take me ages if I was alone
Developers comparing it to pure Claude Code
Jaw Dropping Experience in video reviews

name: GitHub Copilot CLI
description: GitHub Copilot CLI reached general availability on February 25, 2026, after being in public preview since September 2025. A terminal-native AI coding assistant that brings agentic capabilities directly to the command line, enabling developers to use natural language to drive Copilot for building, debugging, and understanding code without leaving the shell.
community_quotes: Since launching in public preview in September 2025, shipped hundreds of improvements shaped by feedback
Available for all paid Copilot subscribers
